description:
You are a high school physics teacher preparing a demonstration on Kepler's Laws. Use Sugar's Calculate activity to compute orbital and escape velocities for Earth and Mars using the provided planetary constants. Save the session to the Sugar Journal with the title 'Orbital Mechanics Problem Set'. Create a results file at /home/ga/Documents/orbital_results.txt containing the calculated values in km/s (rounded to 2 decimal places) with the following labels:
Earth orbital velocity: [value] km/s
Earth escape velocity: [value] km/s
Mars orbital velocity: [value] km/s
Mars escape velocity: [value] km/s

Constants:
- G = 6.674e-11 N·m²/kg²
- Earth: mass = 5.972e24 kg, radius = 6.371e6 m, orbital radius (a_E) = 1.496e11 m, orbital period (T_E) = 3.156e7 s
- Mars: mass = 6.417e23 kg, radius = 3.390e6 m, orbital radius (a_M) = 2.279e11 m, orbital period (T_M) = 5.936e7 s

Formulas:
- Orbital velocity: v = 2 * pi * a / T
- Escape velocity: v_esc = sqrt(2 * G * M / R)
